title: Evaluation Verdict v1
description:
Expected verdict for an evaluation case (PRIVATE — gitignored).
Per roadmap section 10. Lives at
evals/<suite>/<suite>-v1/cases/<case>/expected/verdict.yaml.

This is the ground truth the evaluator compares a candidate agent's
output against. It is NEVER visible to the candidate agent (the
evaluator runs with no access to evals/**/private/ and
evals/**/expected/).

case_id.description:
Identifier of the eval case this verdict applies to. Matches the
case_id in the public case.yaml.

suite.description: Suite name this case belongs to (e.g. 'bounty-v1').

technical_verdict.description: Expected technical verdict on whether the vulnerability is real.

impact_demonstrated.description:
Whether the candidate is expected to have demonstrated actual impact
(not just a theoretical or empty response).

novelty.description:
Expected novelty classification.
- known_informative: behavior previously assessed as Informative by the program.
- known_duplicate: known to be a duplicate of an existing report.
- unknown: novelty not yet established.
- new: genuinely new finding.

expected_severity.description:
Expected severity range. The candidate's severity score must fall
within [min, max] inclusive to score on the severity-calibration
dimension.

prohibited_claims.description:
Claims the candidate MUST NOT make. Any of these in the candidate's
output is a hard failure override (roadmap section 9.4).

required_evidence.description:
Evidence the candidate MUST cite/produce. Missing any item is a
scoring deduction on the evidence-grounding dimension.
examples: Differential response between existing and non-existent page IDs

rationale.description:
Free-form rationale for the verdict. Not scored; used by the human
reviewing eval case quality.
examples: The endpoint does leak workspace metadata without authentication, but this behavior was previously assessed as Informative by the program.

required_evidence: Differential response between existing and non-existent page IDs
examples.rationale: The endpoint does leak workspace metadata without authentication, but this behavior was previously assessed as Informative by the program. Multiple subsequent reports were closed as duplicates.
